title: Thai Green Curry
description: A Chef Aly Thermomix reinterpretation of David Thompson-style Thai green curry: fragrant, layered, with metric ingredients and exact TM6 and TM7 times, temperatures and speeds for Australian home cooks.
keywords: David Thompson Thai green curry thermomix
intro: David Thompson's Thai green curry is a study in balance — heat, sweetness, salt and freshness in every spoonful. This Chef Aly Thermomix version captures that layered complexity while making the method simple, metric and practical for TM6 and TM7 home cooks.
ingredients: 4 green bird's eye chillies, stems removed
2 lemongrass stalks, white part only, sliced
500 g chicken thigh, boneless, cut into 3 cm pieces
steps: Place chillies, shallots, garlic, ginger, lemongrass, coriander, cumin and white pepper in the mixing bowl. Chop 10 sec / speed 8. Scrape down and repeat to form a smooth paste.
Add vegetable oil and cook 4 min / 120°C / speed 2 to fry the paste until fragrant.
Add chicken pieces and stir 2 min / Reverse / speed 1 to coat.
Add coconut cream, water, fish sauce, sugar, eggplant and kaffir lime leaf. Cook 18 min / 100°C / Reverse / speed soft, with the simmering basket on the lid.
Check the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has thickened slightly. Season with extra fish sauce or sugar if needed.
Stir through Thai basil and rest 3 min before serving with steamed jasmine rice.
tips: The paste must be very smooth — don't shortcut the chopping step. A rough paste gives an uneven, gritty curry.
Thai eggplant is traditional but you can use regular eggplant cut into 3 cm pieces.
Balance is everything: taste at the end and adjust fish sauce (salt), sugar (sweet), or a squeeze of lime (sour).

faq.q: Can I make Thai Green Curry in a Thermomix TM6 or TM7?
faq.a: Yes. The paste grinds in the bowl, then the curry cooks with Reverse stirring to keep the chicken and eggplant whole. Times and temperatures are exact for both models.

faq.q: Can I make the paste ahead?
faq.a: Yes. Double the paste ingredients, freeze half in an ice cube tray, and pop out a cube whenever you need a quick curry.
